title: Pervasive Demographic Synchrony Across Holocene Eurasia: Evidence from 154,000 Radiocarbon Dates
abstract: Using 154,466 radiocarbon dates from p3k14c across seven Eurasian regions, we document pervasive positive demographic synchrony (mean detrended r = 0.458, 20/21 pairs significant) that survives six robustness tests: Bartlett correction, block bootstrap, loess detrending, taphonomic correction (r = 0.419), calibration artifact null (p < 0.001), and equal-sample subsampling (r = 0.307). Synchrony is distance-independent (Mantel p = 0.194) but climate-population correlations (mean |r| = 0.086) are 5.3x weaker than inter-regional correlations, suggesting climate forcing alone is insufficient. Peak synchrony at the Late Bronze Age (~2,600-2,950 cal BP) provides quantitative evidence of pan-Eurasian demographic coupling.
novelty_statement: First systematic cross-continental analysis of detrended SPD synchrony with taphonomic correction, calibration artifact control, and equal-sample subsampling. Key surprise: climate forcing is insufficient to explain the observed inter-regional demographic coupling.
